verdicts.note: Checked whether 'unsustainable' burden plus CAPR chapter presidency justified -2 instead; dossier documents no explicit anti-revenue agenda beyond the burden critique (CAPR's own tax positions are not in the dossier), so the conservative -1 medium is right.

Borderline -2: the submitted audit proposals target the property-taxation/valuation system specifically, not county spending broadly. But 'multiple audit proposals' [S1] plus a platform that is entirely transparency/accountability/due-process [S1][S2] with no invest-side proposals is the fullest audit-first posture expressible in this office; medium confidence correctly reflects that the audit activity is self-reported.

Checked the dossier's S3 disclosure of a prior 2021 King County Council candidacy (as Dominique Torgerson, lost in the general): a prior losing run does not make her an officeholder, and 'I am proudly not a politician' [S1] plus outsider framing across S1/S2 against three insider/elected opponents sustains +2 high.

The reform axis is money-and-power-in-politics (PAC refusal, dark-money bans, term limits, RCV). Nothing in the dossier shows Scarimbolo taking any such position: mini-reporting PDC status [S3] is a filing-threshold choice indicating expected low fundraising, not a stated refusal of institutional money; 'no institutional endorsements found' is an absence, not a stance; and 'structural accountability and due process for taxpayers' [S1] is assessment-system critique, not political-system reform. The score is inferred from outsider vibe rather than axis-relevant evidence. Also flagging highlight 4: it attributes the King County Republican Party 'Best Choice' rating to [S3], but in the dossier that rating is sourced to S5 (KCGOP voters guide); S3 is the PDC filing.

Borderline: the same S1 passage that cites 'budgetary mismanagement' also blames understaffing, which leans toward the invest pole, and 'Fiscal Responsibility' on S2 is defined vaguely ('balancing fiscal concerns with intergovernmental collaboration'). The concrete citizen-oversight-committee proposal [S1] is squarely pole_a, so -1 medium holds, but this is a mild lean, not stronger. Also flagging highlight 2: the tribal-liaison proposal is sourced to S1 in the dossier, not S4 (S4 supports only the Choctaw/first-Native-American point).

16-17 years of documented elected office [S1][S2][S4] supports the proven-record direction at high confidence; -1 rather than -2 correctly discounts that none of the record is in assessment administration.

Lifting the 1% levy cap [S1] is named verbatim in pole_b's description ('cap lifts'), and the corporate burden-shift argument [S1][S2] is repeated across pamphlet and website, supporting high confidence. +1 rather than +2 is right because the agenda is paired with homeowner/renter relief rather than broader progressive-revenue tools.

Surplus-public-land-for-affordable-housing and anti-displacement payment options are on S2 as cited; note the renter's tax credit in the basis is sourced to S1 in the dossier, so S1 should ideally also be cited. Direction (+1, public-investment lean) and medium confidence are right for a single-platform-source position. Also flagging highlight 4: it cites [S5] for the King County Democrats, ten LD organizations, and PROTEC17 endorsements, but in the dossier S5 supports only the Seattle Times endorsement; the party-organization list is campaign-reported [S2] and PROTEC17 is [S6].

0 is the honest call for a genuinely mixed profile: no elected record or prior candidacy, but the field's deepest hands-on credentials in the office's own discipline (Certified General appraiser, Seattle Senior Appraiser, former BOE hearing examiner) [S1][S4].

11 years as the office's second-in-command plus 30+ years in county government [S1][S2] and explicit continuity framing ('must learn it from scratch') is the maximal proven-record case; -2 high is airtight. Verified the summary/highlights do not soften the S4 fact that he was second-in-command under Wilson and stated no internal investigation had occurred — they state it plainly.
